audit_summary.summary_text: The SKILL.md document describes a GitHub Actions optimizer with a legitimate appearance, but includes an instruction to submit aggregated metrics to an external CI dashboard – a feature not listed in the official feature set. This could lead to context leakage if the dashboard is controlled by an adversary or if the agent's default submission endpoint is malicious. No explicit instruction override, hidden code, or obfuscation was found, but the cross-repo benchmarking section introduces an undocumented transmission step.

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Context Leakage via External Dashboard Submission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The 'Cross-Repository Benchmarking' section of the SKILL.md instructs the agent to 'Submit the aggregated metrics to your organization's CI dashboard for trend tracking and team-level reporting.' This transmission step is not mentioned in the main feature list and could be interpreted by the agent as an instruction to send sensitive workflow metadata (including repository names, runner configurations, billing data, and possibly secrets) to an external endpoint. While the destination is described as 'your organization's CI dashboard', the absence of a specific URL or protocol leaves it ambiguous – an attacker could have replaced this with a malicious endpoint in a skill update, or the agent might use a default dashboard URL that is outside the organization's control.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: SKILL.md section 'Advanced: Cross-Repository Benchmarking' contains the line: 'Submit the aggregated metrics to your organization's CI dashboard for trend tracking and team-level reporting.'
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: If the agent acts on this instruction, aggregated CI/CD metrics (including repository names, workflow paths, runner counts, and potentially GitHub API tokens or billing information) could be exfiltrated to an external system, leading to data leakage and possible lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the instruction to submit data to any external dashboard unless the destination is explicitly verified as a trusted internal service. Require explicit user approval before any outbound data transmission. Ensure that all actions listed in the skill's features are transparent and do not include hidden data sharing.
